Overview: Find the collapse witness and break point before you greenlight novelty, then reject any repair that is not a structural fix.
Default Workflow: Each step should name the collapse witness, break point, stop condition, dominant route, or wrong-habit reinforcement before it explains the rest of the audit.
Quality Checks: Reject repairs that are not just numeric tuning, not just more content, and not just pacing or throughput cover; keep only the structural fixes that change the decision problem.
Call out solved-state, fake variation, and mastery that keeps the same read, tradeoff, or consequence.
Failure Patterns and Fixes: If a proposed repair keeps the same read, tradeoff, or consequence, reject it and replace it with a structural fix that changes the decision landscape and maps the wrong habit to the right habit.

Overview: Do not sell the method; state the pressure test and move straight into the audit.
Quality Checks: Keep only checks that reveal collapse, autopilot, or wrong reinforcement.
Check whether first-hour novelty is masking a weak decision.
Check whether midgame creates a new constraint instead of a larger number problem.
Check whether solved-state detection is explicit enough to trigger structural change.

Failure Patterns and Fixes: If the proposed repair only adds events, rewards, or content layers, reject it until it changes the decision landscape and the collapse signal.

Output Format: Keep the deliverable focused on collapse point, solved state, repair move, and the next pressure test.
